blocks.text: DTC P1659: DBW power supply relay circuit abnormal (OFF failure)

The DBW system opens and closes the throttle valve under electronic control. The system consists of a throttle actuator built into the throttle body, throttle valve and throttle position sensors 1/2, accelerator pedal position sensors 1/2, DBW relay, and PGM-FI ECU. When the accelerator pedal is operated, the accelerator pedal position sensor is driven to detect the amount of pressure on the accelerator pedal. The throttle valve opening target value is set based on the amount of pressure on the accelerator pedal signaled by the accelerator pedal position sensor, and the throttle actuator is driven to open and close the throttle valve to achieve the target value. The actual throttle valve opening is detected by the throttle opening sensor 1/2 attached to the throttle body. If the DBW relay power supply voltage is not input for more than a set period of time after the PGM-FI ECU turns on the DBW relay in the ignition ON mode, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the DBW relay has failed OFF and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
DBW relay power voltage is not supplied for more than 0.2 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of cord between PGM-FI ECU and DBW relay (DBW RLY OUT line)
Disconnection of code between PGM-FI ECU and DBW relay (DBW RLY CL-line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
